parts.text: Mark Zuckerberg is widely known as the co-founder and CEO of Meta Platforms, the company behind Facebook, which he launched from his Harvard dorm room in 2004. Under his leadership, Facebook rapidly grew into a global social media phenomenon, profoundly reshaping how billions of people connect and communicate worldwide. Today, Zuckerberg continues to steer Meta's vision toward the metaverse, artificial intelligence, and virtual reality, alongside his philanthropic efforts with the Chan Zuckerberg Initiative.

In December 1984, the Bhopal disaster occurred in India when a Union Carbide pesticide plant leaked methyl isocyanate gas, killing thousands instantly and causing long-term health issues for hundreds of thousands more. This catastrophic industrial accident remains one of the world's worst, profoundly influencing environmental regulations and corporate accountability for chemical safety.

parts.text:
# Year: 1984

## Famous Person Bio:

Mark Zuckerberg is widely known as the co-founder and CEO of Meta Platforms, the company behind Facebook, which he launched from his Harvard dorm room in 2004. Under his leadership, Facebook rapidly grew into a global social media phenomenon, profoundly reshaping how billions of people connect and communicate worldwide. Today, Zuckerberg continues to steer Meta's vision toward the metaverse, artificial intelligence, and virtual reality, alongside his philanthropic efforts with the Chan Zuckerberg Initiative.

## Historical Event:

In December 1984, the Bhopal disaster occurred in India when a Union Carbide pesticide plant leaked methyl isocyanate gas, killing thousands instantly and causing long-term health issues for hundreds of thousands more. This catastrophic industrial accident remains one of the world's worst, profoundly influencing environmental regulations and corporate accountability for chemical safety.
